# Component Metadata Rules
*or what to Do When Metadata Changes the Outcome of Resolution*

This scenario demonstrates fixing bad metadata with Gradle rules.

#### Dependency Graph
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 4.4
graph LR
    Root[Project Root] --> LibA[lib-a:1.0.0]
    LibA --> LibB[lib-b:1.0.0]
    LibB -.-> LibB_Fixed[lib-b:2.0.0]
```

#### Gradle (Groovy DSL)

```{.groovy include="/demos/gradle-demo/scenario-12-metadata/build.gradle" snippet="s12-deps"}
```

#### Expected Intuition
"I can fix it without waiting for upstream."

#### Actual Resolution Results
*   **Gradle**: `lib-b` becomes `2.0.0`.

#### Classpath Reality
`2.0.0`.

#### Why This Happens
Rule modifies metadata in-memory.

#### How Developers Commonly "Fix" This
Exclusions + explicit dependency.

#### Safer Ways to Take Control
Metadata rules are safer as they apply globally.

#### Signals to Watch For
N/A

#### How This Scales in Real Systems
Powerful for patching ecosystem issues.

#### Key Takeaway
You can patch metadata on the fly.

#### Related Scenarios
N/A
